questions.q: What does the abbreviation CBFC stand for in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: CBFC stands for the Central Board of Film Certification, the statutory body that certifies films for public exhibition in India.

questions.q: Under which Indian law does the Central Board of Film Certification operate?
options: The Press and Registration of Books Act, 1867
questions.explanation: The CBFC certifies films under the Cinematograph Act, 1952, which provides the statutory framework for film certification in India.

questions.q: By what popular nickname is the Central Board of Film Certification commonly known in India?
questions.explanation: The CBFC is popularly known as the Censor Board, reflecting its earlier name and its role in reviewing films.

questions.q: What was the Central Board of Film Certification originally called before it was renamed?
questions.explanation: The body was originally the Central Board of Film Censors before being renamed the Central Board of Film Certification in 1983.

questions.q: In which year was the Central Board of Film Censors renamed the Central Board of Film Certification?
questions.explanation: The body was renamed the Central Board of Film Certification in 1983, shifting emphasis from censorship to certification.

questions.q: Which CBFC certificate category in India indicates a film is suitable for unrestricted public exhibition?
questions.explanation: The 'U' (Universal) certificate signifies a film is suitable for unrestricted public exhibition and viewing by all ages.

questions.q: What does the 'A' certificate issued by the CBFC mean for a film in India?
questions.explanation: The 'A' certificate restricts a film to adult audiences, meaning viewers must be 18 years or older.

questions.q: Which CBFC certificate category restricts a film to a specialised class of audience such as doctors or scientists?
questions.explanation: The 'S' certificate restricts a film to a specialised audience such as doctors, scientists or other specific professional groups.

questions.q: What does the traditional 'U/A' certificate from the CBFC advise audiences in India?
options: The film is for adults only
The film is for a specialised audience
Parental guidance is advised for children under 12
The film is unrestricted with no caution
questions.explanation: The traditional 'U/A' certificate allows unrestricted exhibition but advises parental guidance for children below the age of 12.

questions.q: Which Union ministry of the Government of India administers the Central Board of Film Certification?
questions.explanation: The CBFC functions under the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting of the Government of India.

questions.q: In which city is the headquarters of the Central Board of Film Certification located?
questions.explanation: The CBFC's headquarters is located in Mumbai, with several regional offices across India.

questions.q: Who heads the Central Board of Film Certification?
questions.explanation: The CBFC is headed by a Chairperson, supported by board members appointed by the central government.

questions.q: A 2016 committee set up by the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting to recommend reforms to film certification in India was chaired by which filmmaker?
questions.explanation: The committee, set up by the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting in 2016, was chaired by filmmaker Shyam Benegal and recommended that the CBFC act mainly as a certification rather than censoring body.

questions.q: Which appellate body, since abolished in 2021, once heard appeals against CBFC certification decisions?
questions.explanation: The Film Certification Appellate Tribunal (FCAT) heard appeals against CBFC decisions until it was abolished in 2021.

questions.q: Which filmmaker served as Chairperson of the CBFC from 2015 to 2017 before being replaced?
questions.explanation: Pahlaj Nihalani served as CBFC Chairperson from 2015 to 2017, a tenure marked by several controversial certification decisions.

questions.q: Which veteran actress chaired the CBFC from 2004 to 2011, becoming a prominent face of the board?
questions.explanation: Actress Sharmila Tagore served as Chairperson of the CBFC from 2004 to 2011.

questions.q: Which lyricist and writer was appointed Chairperson of the CBFC in 2017, succeeding Pahlaj Nihalani?
questions.explanation: Lyricist and adman Prasoon Joshi was appointed CBFC Chairperson in 2017, succeeding Pahlaj Nihalani.

questions.q: The 2023 amendment to India's certification framework introduced finer age-based subcategories of which existing CBFC certificate?
questions.explanation: The Cinematograph (Amendment) Act, 2023 split the U/A certificate into age-based subcategories such as U/A 7+, U/A 13+ and U/A 16+.

questions.q: Which 2023 legislation amended India's certification law to add age-based subcategories and curb film piracy?
questions.explanation: The Cinematograph (Amendment) Act, 2023 introduced age-based U/A subcategories and stronger anti-piracy provisions.

questions.q: What is the primary function of the Central Board of Film Certification in India?
options: To certify films for public exhibition
To award the National Film Awards
questions.explanation: The CBFC's primary function is to certify films for public exhibition, assigning categories such as U, U/A, A and S.

questions.q: Members of the CBFC's examining and advisory panels who review films are commonly referred to by which term?
questions.explanation: Films are reviewed by examining committees made up of CBFC panel members drawn from various walks of life.

questions.q: The landmark 1970 Supreme Court case K. A. Abbas v. Union of India dealt with the constitutional validity of what?
options: The income tax on film studios
questions.explanation: In K. A. Abbas v. Union of India (1970), the Supreme Court upheld the constitutional validity of pre-censorship of films.

questions.q: Indian films that have not received a CBFC certificate are generally not permitted to do what?
options: Be entered in private writing contests
questions.explanation: Under the Cinematograph Act, a film must hold a valid CBFC certificate before it can be exhibited publicly in Indian theatres.

questions.q: Which 1994 feature film, whose CBFC clearance was challenged before the Supreme Court in the 1996 Bobby Art International case, shaped important free-speech principles for film certification?
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court's 1996 ruling in Bobby Art International v. Om Pal Singh Hoon concerned Shekhar Kapur's film Bandit Queen and shaped certification jurisprudence.

questions.q: In the 1980s, which letter category did the CBFC use for films restricted to adult viewers?
questions.explanation: The CBFC has used the 'A' (Adults) category for adult-restricted films, rather than ratings like X, R or M used elsewhere.

questions.q: The Khosla Committee, which examined film censorship in India, submitted its report in which decade?
questions.explanation: The Khosla Committee, chaired by Justice G. D. Khosla, examined film censorship and submitted its report in 1969.

questions.q: Before a film is certified, the CBFC may require filmmakers to make what kind of changes?
options: Cuts or modifications to certain scenes
Changes to the film's box-office price
questions.explanation: The CBFC can require cuts or modifications to specific scenes or dialogue as a condition of granting a certificate.

questions.q: Does the certification authority of the CBFC under the Cinematograph Act extend to films intended for theatrical release in India?
options: No, it only covers television serials
Yes, it covers films for public theatrical exhibition
No, it only covers radio broadcasts
questions.explanation: The CBFC certifies films, including feature films and trailers, intended for public theatrical exhibition in India.

questions.q: Which emblem represents the Central Board of Film Certification on its Indian cinema card describing it as India's censor board?
questions.explanation: The CBFC, as a protective certifying authority, is represented by a shield emblem on its Indian cinema card.

questions.q: When a Hindi or regional film begins, the certificate card shown on screen at the start is issued by which body?
options: The Film and Television Institute of India
The Central Board of Film Certification
questions.explanation: The certification card displayed at the start of an Indian film is issued by the Central Board of Film Certification.
